programme.title: The Blue Realm Ep110: The Ship Sinkers

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: In the Season 2 premiere of Surviving Mann you'll meet The Black Team and The Blue Team—12 tough-as-nails contestants who will take on everything Don could throw at them even after seeing Season 1! Co-host Sheriff Mark Lamb jumps in to bring advanced law enforcement training tactics & strategies to the evolution line-up.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: The day begins with a powerful dose of perspective as contestants hear real-life breaching stories from American heroes—reminders of the stakes they’re preparing for. The team is then introduced to a concealed firearm headrest safe designed for rapid-response vehicle defense. Next, it’s straight into the action with a preliminary course of fire using GreenLight sights, where top performers are pushed even further with a bonus round to test speed, accuracy, and composure under pressure. The intensity ramps up during a sentry scenario featuring SABRE CO₂ non-lethal cartridges, putting their stealth and judgment to the test. But just as the dust begins to settle, a new CIA briefing drops—and it’s the most dire yet. With mission reality closing in fast, the contestants close the day with a crash course in S.E.R.E. (Survival, Evasion, Resistance, and Escape) training, rounding out their classroom combat readiness with hard-earned grit.

programme.title: All Stars
programme.desc: It’s go time. The mission day has come and the All-Stars have to rendezvous on a nearby island to pick up their supplies. When it comes to gear, Don pulls out all the stops to ensure mission success. From water jets and state-of-the-art fins to maximize speed, to finding a creative way to keep his team lethally armed without breaking any laws.. Don has a few surprises in store for the All-Stars.

programme.title: Trans-Saharan Highway | Brilliant Corners: Algeria, Episode 3

Back in Algiers, Sam, Erwan, and Dihya visit one of the country's leading digital agencies to discuss their journey and go for one last surf in some perfect longboard waves. programme.title: Science on the Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: Join host Philippe Cousteau Jr., grandson of legendary explorer Jacques Cousteau, as he journeys to the most spectacular places on the planet. In this episode, Cousteau dives off the coast of Australia to explore the Great Barrier Reef. He then heads to James Cook University to get an up-close look at some of the country's deadliest creatures. Philippe ends his journey by visiting the National Sea Simulator at the Australian Institute of Marine Science.

programme.title: Caught On Clips
programme.desc: The wildest, wackiest, and most jaw-dropping videos. From dangerous severe weather, to heart-stopping close calls and adorable animal antics - all caught on camera.

programme.title: Science on the Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: Join host Philippe Cousteau Jr., grandson of legendary explorer Jacques Cousteau, as he journeys to the most spectacular places on the planet. In this episode, Cousteau dives off the coast of Australia to explore the Great Barrier Reef. He then heads to James Cook University to get an up-close look at some of the country's deadliest creatures. Philippe ends his journey by visiting the National Sea Simulator at the Australian Institute of Marine Science.

programme.title: Science on the Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: Join host Philippe Cousteau Jr., grandson of legendary explorer Jacques Cousteau, as he journeys to the most spectacular places on the planet. In this episode, Cousteau dives off the coast of Australia to explore the Great Barrier Reef. He then heads to James Cook University to get an up-close look at some of the country's deadliest creatures. Philippe ends his journey by visiting the National Sea Simulator at the Australian Institute of Marine Science.

programme.title: Science on the Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: Join host Philippe Cousteau Jr., grandson of legendary explorer Jacques Cousteau, as he journeys to the most spectacular places on the planet. In this episode, Cousteau dives off the coast of Australia to explore the Great Barrier Reef. He then heads to James Cook University to get an up-close look at some of the country's deadliest creatures. Philippe ends his journey by visiting the National Sea Simulator at the Australian Institute of Marine Science.

programme.title: Science on the Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: Join host Philippe Cousteau Jr., grandson of legendary explorer Jacques Cousteau, as he journeys to the most spectacular places on the planet. In this episode, Cousteau dives off the coast of Australia to explore the Great Barrier Reef. He then heads to James Cook University to get an up-close look at some of the country's deadliest creatures. Philippe ends his journey by visiting the National Sea Simulator at the Australian Institute of Marine Science.

programme.title: Science on the Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: Join host Philippe Cousteau Jr., grandson of legendary explorer Jacques Cousteau, as he journeys to the most spectacular places on the planet. In this episode, Cousteau dives off the coast of Australia to explore the Great Barrier Reef. He then heads to James Cook University to get an up-close look at some of the country's deadliest creatures. Philippe ends his journey by visiting the National Sea Simulator at the Australian Institute of Marine Science.
